question:
Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..3, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).

C = [[-3, -0.5, -1.5], [-0.5, 3, 0], [-1.5, 0, -1]]
A_1 = [[1, 1, -1.5], [1, 3, 0.5], [-1.5, 0.5, 3]]
A_2 = [[2, 2, -1], [2, -3, 2], [-1, 2, 3]]
A_3 = [[1, -2, 0.5], [-2, 3, 0.5], [0.5, 0.5, 3]]
b_1 = 19.0, b_2 = -20.0, b_3 = 49.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?

Consider the semidefinite program over a symmetric 3x3 matrix X:
Minimize <C, X> subject to <A_i, X> = b_i for i=1..3, and X is positive semidefinite (X ⪰ 0).

C = [[1, -0.5, 2], [-0.5, 2, 0.5], [2, 0.5, 1]]
A_1 = [[3, 0.5, -0.5], [0.5, -1, -0.5], [-0.5, -0.5, -2]]
A_2 = [[3, 0.5, 0], [0.5, 3, -1], [0, -1, 1]]
A_3 = [[-3, 1, 0], [1, 1, 0], [0, 0, 3]]
b_1 = -22.0, b_2 = 34.0, b_3 = 34.0

What is the minimum value of <C, X> (rounded to 3 decimals)?
